action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 24's model-soup TTA scored 0.2493 AP, below the best test AP of 0.2533 achieved in iteration 21. Analysis: Iteration 21 (load best+ema separately, 32 TTA runs each, average probabilities, batch_size=1024) is the sweet spot. Subsequent changes—doubling TTA to 64 per model, logit averaging, and weight-space soup—all hurt generalization. With only ~7.7 minutes left, training is impossible. The highest-probability zero-cost recovery is to revert predict.py to the exact iteration 21 logic while keeping batch_size=2048 for faster inference, which should replicate ~0.2533 AP and finish safely within the time budget.
